question: When can the content module depend on a learner-level recording instead of fixed chunks?
current_answer: Not yet for open content recognition. The system can evaluate tajweed rule modules on learner recordings, but the content-recognition path still needs chunk/segment-level supervision because open recognition of new text is not reliable enough.

current_production_content_path.name: tuned_chunked_content_baseline
current_production_content_path.status: best official content baseline, still chunk-based

best_bridge_candidate.name: distilled_curriculum_expanded_vocab
best_bridge_candidate.status: preserves old behavior but does not learn new aligned chunks enough

separate_expanded_recognizer_probe.status: not ready; even a separate recognizer does not yet generalize to held-out expanded text

minimum_chunk_level_gate: Open decoder, zero held-out lexicon coverage
Strict text-held-out exact match >= current tuned baseline
Strict text-held-out char accuracy >= current tuned baseline
New aligned chunk held-out char accuracy should be substantially above the current failed probes
next_gate_after_chunk_level: Sliding or segmentation inference over full learner audio
Aggregate chunk predictions into a verse/phrase-level transcript
Evaluate on learner recordings whose text was not in the training phrase list

plain_english_explanation: Chunks are currently the safe training/evaluation unit because each chunk has a known target text and reliable timing.
A learner-level content model would receive a longer recording and infer the spoken text without being given a tiny list of allowed answers.
We can only trust that once the model first proves it can recognize new chunk texts reliably.
Our latest experiments show we are close to preserving the old chunk baseline while expanding vocabulary, but we are not yet recognizing new aligned text well enough.
recommended_next_step: Increase high-quality aligned content data and/or prototype an adapter or dual-head model that keeps the old chunk head stable while training a separate expanded-text head.
